title: Notes from the Rafters
synopsis: A dusty attic trunk tells the story of Sable, a musical baby seal, who gets stuck in the attic with a grumpy bat during a three-day rainstorm. Through four seasons of slapstick mishaps and unexpected friendship, they discover that home isn't where you are, but who you're with.

pages.text: The rain started on Tuesday and forgot to stop. I'm just a dusty old trunk in the corner, but I see everything that happens in this attic. Today, something extraordinary tumbled through my lid with a tremendous THUD and a musical "Ooooh!" A sleek baby seal with silver-grey fur, huge dark liquid eyes, and long silver whiskers had somehow flopped right into my belly! "How did I get here?" warbled the little seal, looking around with those enormous eyes. "One minute I was sliding down the drainpipe, and now..." She tried to climb out, but her flippers were too slippery. Every time she pushed up, she slid back down with a WHOMP! "Oh, barnacles!" she huffed. From the rafters came a cranky screech: "QUIET DOWN THERE!" A grumpy bat hung upside down, glaring. "Some of us are trying to sleep!" The baby seal blinked up at him. "I'm Sable! I make music!" To prove it, she clapped her flippers together. The sound echoed like thunder, and three boxes of Christmas ornaments crashed down. SMASH! TINKLE! CRASH! The bat's fur stood straight up. "MUSIC?! That's not music, that's a tornado!" But Sable was already humming a happy tune, even as tinsel rained down like silver snow.

illustration.description: Treasure map style illustration with aged parchment background and sepia tones. A sleek baby seal with silver-grey fur, huge dark liquid eyes, and long silver whiskers sits inside an open trunk. Compass roses mark the corners as Christmas ornaments cascade down like jewel accents. A grumpy bat hangs from rafters drawn as bold explorers' map elements. Dotted trails show the path of falling decorations.
illustration.alt: Sable the baby seal sits in a trunk while ornaments fall around her in the attic

pages.text: By summer, the rain was still drumming on the roof like a thousand tiny dancers. I watched as Sable tried again and again to escape from inside me. "Maybe if I bounce..." she said, wiggling her silver whiskers thoughtfully. She rocked back and forth, building up speed. BOING! She shot up like a fuzzy cannonball, flippers flailing wildly! But instead of landing gracefully, she crashed into a stack of board games. AVALANCHE! Monopoly money fluttered everywhere like confetti. Scrabble tiles pelted down like hail. "A-R-G-H spells ARGH!" screeched the bat as a tile bonked his head. Sable landed back in me with a tremendous SPLASH in a puddle that had formed from the leak above. "That was fun!" she giggled, shaking water from her whiskers. The bat swooped down, soaking wet and furious. "Fun?! You've turned this place into a swimming pool!" But something strange happened. As Sable hummed a gentle, bluesy tune, the bat's wings began to tap-tap-tap against the trunk's edge. "Stop that!" he grumbled, but his wings kept tapping. "I don't like music!" Sable grinned, her huge dark eyes twinkling. "Your wings say otherwise!" The bat tried to look stern, but a tiny smile crept across his face.

illustration.description: Treasure map style illustration on aged parchment showing a sleek baby seal with silver-grey fur, huge dark liquid eyes, and long silver whiskers splashing in a puddle inside the trunk. Board game pieces float around like jewel accents. A wet bat with tapping wings is drawn in bold explorers' style. Compass roses frame the chaos, with dotted trails showing the trajectory of flying game pieces.
illustration.alt: Sable splashes in the trunk while board game pieces fly everywhere and the bat gets soaked

pages.text: When fall colors should have been painting the world outside, all we could see was grey rain streaming down the tiny attic window. Sable had been practicing her music for weeks now, and it was... well, let's just say the neighborhood dogs had started howling along from three blocks away. "Watch this!" Sable announced, balancing a dusty harmonica on her nose. She took a deep breath and... HOOOOONK! The sound was so loud that every spider in the attic fell from their webs at once. They landed on the bat, who was now hanging directly above me. "SPIDERS!" he shrieked, doing a frantic upside-down dance. "GET THEM OFF! GET THEM OFF!" Sable tried to help by clapping her flippers in rhythm, but this only made more things fall down - old photo albums, a bowling ball, and somehow a rubber chicken that went SQUEAK every time it bounced. "This is chaos!" wailed the bat, still covered in confused spiders. But Sable had started singing a silly song about spiders doing the tango, and despite himself, the bat began to laugh. "You're completely ridiculous," he chuckled, finally brushing off the last spider. "But that song actually wasn't terrible." From my cozy corner, I could see something beautiful happening - they were becoming friends.

illustration.description: Treasure map style illustration with sepia tones and aged parchment texture. A sleek baby seal with silver-grey fur, huge dark liquid eyes, and long silver whiskers balances a harmonica on her nose inside the trunk. Spiders drawn as tiny jewel accents rain down on a laughing bat. Bold explorers' style details show falling photo albums and a rubber chicken. Compass roses mark the corners with dotted trails following the chaos.
illustration.alt: Sable plays harmonica while spiders fall on the bat and various objects tumble down in the attic

chapters.title: Winter: Home in the Highest Place

pages.text: The rain finally stopped on a crisp winter morning, but something wonderful had happened. Sable no longer tried to escape from me. Instead, she had made me into the coziest home imaginable, complete with pillow forts and a little stage made from old books. The bat - who we learned was named Forte - had moved his sleeping spot to hang right above me. "You know," said Forte, munching on a cookie crumb, "I never thought I'd enjoy having a roommate." Sable was practicing a new song, this one soft and sweet like lullabies. "I never thought I'd enjoy being stuck anywhere," she admitted, her silver whiskers twitching happily. "But this feels like home now." They had turned the whole attic into their playground. Forte would swoop around doing aerial tricks while Sable provided the soundtrack. Sometimes they'd have tea parties using old teacups (carefully, after the Great Crashing Incident of November). Other times they'd put on concerts for me and the mice who lived in the walls. "We make a pretty good team," said Forte, hanging contentedly upside down. Sable grinned, her huge dark eyes sparkling with joy. "The best kind of family is the one you choose," she said, and began to sing a song about friendship that was so beautiful, even the dust motes seemed to dance. From my spot in the corner, holding them both safely inside, I couldn't have agreed more.

illustration.description: Treasure map style illustration on aged parchment with warm sepia tones and jewel accents. A sleek baby seal with silver-grey fur, huge dark liquid eyes, and long silver whiskers sits cozily in the trunk surrounded by pillow forts. A happy bat hangs above, drawn in bold explorers' style. Compass roses frame the scene as dotted trails show their play areas. The attic is transformed into a magical home with hand-drawn map details.
illustration.alt: Sable and Forte have made the attic into a cozy home with the trunk as their base

coverPrompt.description: A treasure map style illustration showing a dusty attic drawn like an ancient map, with compass roses in the corners and sepia tones. Dotted trails lead to various attic treasures. In the center, a sleek baby seal with silver-grey fur, huge dark liquid eyes, and long silver whiskers sits next to an old trunk. Bold explorers' sketches of furniture and cobwebs frame the edges with jewel accents.
coverPrompt.alt: Treasure map style cover showing an attic with Sable the baby seal

questions.question: How does Sable first end up in the trunk?
questions.explanation: Sable mentions she was sliding down the drainpipe before she ended up in the attic trunk.

questions.question: What is the bat's name?
questions.explanation: The bat tells Sable his name is Forte in the winter chapter.

questions.question: What happens when Sable plays the harmonica?
questions.explanation: The loud harmonica sound makes all the spiders in the attic fall from their webs onto the bat.

questions.question: How long does the rainstorm last?
questions.explanation: The story mentions it's a three-day rainstorm that keeps them stuck in the attic.

questions.question: What does Sable say about family at the end?
choices: The best kind of family is the one you choose
questions.explanation: Sable says 'The best kind of family is the one you choose' when talking about her friendship with Forte.
